title: WOS Kernel Provenance Log Document
description: A WOS Kernel Provenance Log export document per Kernel §8 (Provenance). Carries an append-only array of persisted provenance records emitted by runtime execution. Facts-tier records are normative in the kernel; higher tiers reuse the same envelope with `auditLayer` set accordingly. State-transition records whose transitionTags include 'determination' MUST carry a caseFileSnapshot so notices, appeals, and override reviews can reproduce the exact factual basis used by the decision. This schema validates the post-append export shape -- not unstamped in-memory constructors.

provenanceLog.description: Ordered sequence of persisted provenance records emitted during a workflow run. Records are append-only and their order matches emission order; consumers MUST preserve ordering when re-exporting.

FactsTierRecord.description: A persisted WOS provenance record emitted by runtime execution. Facts-tier semantics are normative in the kernel; higher tiers reuse the same envelope and advertise their tier through `auditLayer`. State transition records whose transitionTags include 'determination' MUST include a caseFileSnapshot so later notices, appeals, and override reviews can reproduce the exact factual basis used by the decision.

id.description: TypeID-structured identifier for this facts-tier provenance record. Reserved family prefix: `prov`.

recordKind.description: Provenance record discriminator. For determination snapshot enforcement, the relevant value is 'stateTransition'. Other record kinds MAY also carry caseFileSnapshot for implementation-specific diagnostics.

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 timestamp stamped by the runtime append path before persistence.

actorId.description: Identifier of the actor who triggered the record, when the processor can name one.

fromState.description: Source lifecycle state for transition-oriented records.

toState.description: Target lifecycle state for transition-oriented records.

event.description: Triggering event or reserved event literal carried by the record kind.

auditLayer.description: Audit tier for the persisted/exported record. Facts-tier records are normative in the kernel; higher layers reuse the same envelope and set a different tier literal.

actorType.description: Actor classification when the processor can resolve one canonically. Reserved literals are `human`, `system`, and `agent`; extension values MUST use an `x-` prefix.

lifecycleState.description: Lifecycle state at the time of the action when the processor can determine it canonically.

definitionVersion.description: Version of the governing Kernel Document stamped at append time.

inputs.description: Input entity references used by this activity.

outputs.description: Output entity references generated by this activity.

transitionTags.description: Semantic tags copied from the firing transition when this record represents a state transition. The presence of 'determination' makes caseFileSnapshot mandatory.

items.description: One semantic transition tag copied from the Kernel transition definition, such as 'determination' for consequential decisions or 'review' for human review steps.

canonicalEventHash.description: Lowercase hex rendering of the 32-byte Trellis `canonical_event_hash` stamped after custody admission, when present.

CapabilityInvocationRecord.description: Shape constraint layered on Facts-tier provenance records emitted by the AI §3.3.1 capability-invocation path. A record representing a precondition-blocked invocation (`recordKind == "capabilityInvocation"` AND `data.invocationBlocked == true`) MUST carry `outcome: "preconditionNotSatisfied"` so audit tooling can distinguish a declarative precondition gate from an agent failure. Every provenance log validated against this kernel schema participates in the MUST via `FactsTierRecord.allOf`; records with other `recordKind` values or absent `data.invocationBlocked` fall outside the guard and are unaffected.

recordKind.description: Provenance record discriminator. `capabilityInvocation` selects records emitted by the AI §3.3.1 capability-invocation path (whether the invocation proceeded, was blocked by a precondition, or failed post-invocation).

data.description: Record payload for a capability-invocation provenance entry. Carries the AI §3.3.1 `invocationBlocked` flag plus any additional context the processor chooses to record (capability id, resolved precondition value, expression source, etc.).

invocationBlocked.description: True when the processor declined to invoke the agent because a capability precondition evaluated to `false` or a non-boolean (AI Integration §3.3.1). The processor MUST then fall through to the fallback chain (AI §8). False (or absent) means the capability was invoked.

x-lm.intent: Signal that a capability precondition blocked agent invocation; pairs with outcome=preconditionNotSatisfied to distinguish declarative gates from agent failures

CaseCreatedRecord.description: Shape constraint layered on Facts-tier provenance records that mark the governed-case boundary. The kernel reserves the `caseCreated` kind and the canonical event literal `case.created`, and it requires the created governed-case reference in `data.caseRef`. The kernel does not freeze the rest of the binding-specific `data` keys; bindings may carry different evidence references inside `data` as long as the record still identifies the created governed case.

IntakeAcceptedRecord.description: Shape constraint layered on Facts-tier provenance records emitted when runtime intake acceptance succeeds. Accepted intake creates or updates governed-case handling, so the record MUST carry the canonical event literal `case.intake.accepted`, binding/intake context in `data`, the accepted case disposition, the canonical governed-case reference, and at least one governed-case output reference.

IntakeRejectedRecord.description: Shape constraint layered on Facts-tier provenance records emitted when runtime intake acceptance rejects a handoff. Rejection records remain detached from governed case mutation and therefore do not require governed-case outputs.

IntakeDeferredRecord.description: Shape constraint layered on Facts-tier provenance records emitted when runtime intake acceptance defers a handoff. Deferred records preserve intake context and reason code without creating or updating governed case state.

CaseCreatedData.description: Kernel-owned minimum payload for a governed-case boundary record. `caseRef` identifies the created governed case; bindings may add their own evidence references alongside it.

caseRef.description: Governed-case reference created or acknowledged by the boundary event.

IntakeDecisionData.description: Minimum runtime-owned payload for non-accepted intake decision provenance. The kernel fixes the intake identity, binding discriminator, and case intent fields so exports and offline verifiers can trace the rejection or deferral back to a concrete handoff. Bindings or host profiles may add more fields.

binding.description: Binding discriminator that interpreted the handoff, such as `formspec`.

intakeId.description: Stable intake identity used for replay and provenance correlation.

caseIntent.description: Binding-derived intent before host policy chose attach, create, reject, or defer.

code.description: Machine-readable rejection or deferral code when intake was not accepted.

IntakeAcceptedData.description: Kernel-owned minimum payload for an accepted intake decision. Accepted intake records MUST identify the runtime's final case disposition and canonical governed-case reference so exports and offline verifiers can bind the intake handoff to the governed case that was created or attached.

caseDisposition.description: Final accepted case disposition when the runtime accepted the handoff.

caseRef.description: Governed-case reference selected by the runtime when acceptance attaches or creates a case.

definitionUrl.description: Kernel definition URL used when acceptance created a governed case.

definitionVersion.description: Kernel definition version used when acceptance created a governed case.

SignatureAffirmationRecord.description: Shape constraint layered on Facts-tier provenance records emitted by the Signature Profile. A `recordKind == "signatureAffirmation"` record MUST carry the signer, role, document, hash, timestamp, identity-binding, consent, provider, ceremony, profile, Formspec response, and custody eligibility fields required by Signature Profile §2.8.

recordKind.description: `signatureAffirmation` selects records emitted when a Signature Profile signing act is accepted.

SignatureAffirmationData.description: Required payload for a Signature Profile affirmation provenance record. Identity-binding and consent-reference shapes are provider-neutral so Formspec and external ceremony adapters can map into the same WOS evidence record.

signerId.description: Stable signer identifier from the ceremony context or assigned WOS actor.

roleId.description: Signature Profile role id that was satisfied by this accepted signing act.

role.description: Signature Profile role literal that defines the signer ceremony semantics.

documentId.description: Signature Profile document id whose declared digest was affirmed by the signer.

documentHash.description: Digest of the document bytes that the signer affirmed during the ceremony.

documentHashAlgorithm.description: Digest algorithm used to compute documentHash for the affirmed document.

signedAt.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the signer accepted the signing act and consent.

signatureProvider.description: Provider or adapter that supplied the signing ceremony evidence.

ceremonyId.description: Provider or adapter ceremony identifier for the accepted signing session.

profileRef.description: URI reference to the Signature Profile used for this signing act.

profileKey.description: Package-local Signature Profile key used for this signing act.

formspecResponseRef.description: URI reference to the canonical Formspec response that supplied signature evidence.

custodyHookEligible.description: SignatureAffirmation records are eligible for WOS custodyHook admission.

SignatureIdentityBinding.description: Provider-neutral identity-binding evidence for a signature affirmation.

method.description: Authentication method used to bind the signer identity to the signing act.

assuranceLevel.description: Assurance strength reached by the identity-binding evidence.

providerRef.description: URI reference to the identity or signature provider, when used.

externalAttestationRef.description: URI reference to an external identity attestation, when one exists.

SignatureConsentReference.description: Consent text/version and response evidence paths used to prove signing intent.

consentTextRef.description: URI reference to the consent text accepted by the signer during the ceremony.

consentVersion.description: Version of the consent text accepted by the signer and recorded as evidence.

acceptedAtPath.description: Path to timestamp evidence in the Formspec response or case file.

affirmationPath.description: Path to the explicit affirmation evidence that proves signing intent.

ProvenanceOutcome.description: Outcome literal recorded on a Facts-tier provenance record when the processor completes (or declines to complete) an action. Open enum: the listed values are reserved across all tiers; vendor extensions MUST use an `x-` prefix to avoid future conflicts. Reserved literals currently defined: `preconditionNotSatisfied` (AI Integration §3.3.1 -- emitted when a capability precondition FEL expression evaluates to false or non-boolean, blocking agent invocation and falling through to the fallback chain) and `convergenceCapReached` (Runtime §10.3 -- emitted when continuous-mode re-evaluation hits the 100-cycle convergence cap for a single triggering mutation).

CaseFileSnapshot.description: Canonical case-file snapshot captured immediately before a determination-tagged transition fires. The value is the factual input to the determination; jcsCanonical and sha256 make the snapshot stable and tamper-evident for notices, appeals, and audit export.

value.description: Raw case-file JSON value observed immediately before the determination transition fires. This value may be an object, array, string, number, boolean, or null depending on the processor's case-state representation.

jcsCanonical.description: Canonical JSON string for value using JCS (RFC 8785) canonicalization semantics. Identical case-file state MUST produce byte-identical canonical strings.

sha256.description: Lowercase SHA-256 hex digest of jcsCanonical. This digest links the human-readable snapshot to tamper-evident exports without requiring downstream systems to reserialize value.
